findings.evidence: `rg -n "units_sq_index_le_of_finrank_eq_one|units_sq_index_rat_le_two|card_units_elementaryTwoQuotient.*eq_one|card_units_elementaryTwoQuotient.*rat" TauCeti/NumberTheory/EffectiveBounds/UnitSquaresCorollaries.lean` shows index declarations at lines 63 and 70, with no quotient `eq_one` or rational counterpart.
findings.fix: Add parallel quotient corollaries, e.g. `card_units_elementaryTwoQuotient_le_of_finrank_eq_one` and `card_units_elementaryTwoQuotient_rat_le_two`, or remove the rational/degree-one index specializations if they are not part of the intended surface.
findings.issue: The file exposes the sharp rational/degree-one index bounds `units_sq_index_le_of_finrank_eq_one` and `units_sq_index_rat_le_two`, but the elementary-2 quotient family skips the corresponding sharp `≤ 2` forms; consumers of the quotient API only get the weaker `≤ 4` specialization from `card_units_elementaryTwoQuotient_le_of_finrank_le_two`.

summary: The new corollary layer is mostly minimal, but the public quotient API is not parallel with the rational/degree-one index specializations it exposes.
